claim: Single-file agents with Astral UV enable inline dependency declaration, eliminating dependency management overhead while keeping agents portable and self-contained
actionable: Use Astral UV's inline dependency syntax at the top of Python scripts; agents become portable, runnable files with no external dependency management

claim: Always request reasoning in LLM tool arguments (via Pydantic models); logging reasoning at every step enables understanding of agent decision-making and debugging
actionable: Add a 'reasoning' field to every tool argument schema; log and display reasoning output for each agent step for transparency and error diagnosis

claim: Pre-verification (run test SQL before final SQL) is as important as post-verification; agents can validate solutions internally before returning results
actionable: Design agents with verification tools before final action; e.g., test SQL query execution before creating final tables or modifying data

claim: Agents automatically make trade-offs within compute budget; they prioritize main objectives (e.g., test pass rate) and may skip less critical steps unless explicitly guided
actionable: Set explicit compute limits (e.g., 10 loops); understand that agents will skip verification or architectural concerns if the main goal doesn't require them

claim: AI coding with prompt chains (architect + editor in two passes) catches missed changes and refines implementation better than single-pass generation
actionable: Use multi-pass AI coding: first pass with architect (reasoning model), second pass with editor; reflection step catches 30-50% more required changes

claim: Scaling compute (longer prompt chains, multiple passes) consistently improves code generation quality for complex refactoring and architecture updates
actionable: When refactoring with AI, run multiple passes (architect → editor → reflection → editor again); more passes = better coverage of edge cases

claim: Agents are elongated prompt chains targeting specific domain problems; adding compute (more loops, higher reasoning effort) extends the chain and solves harder problems
actionable: Think of agents as extended prompts; add compute (loops, reasoning effort) for harder problems; this is how deep research tools work with 5-30 minute runtimes

claim: Lean single-file agent patterns enable rapid iteration without over-investment in specific tools that may become obsolete or replaced by big tech
actionable: Build domain-specific agents (e.g., SQL agent, web scraper) as single files; avoid deep dependency on specific platforms that big tech might disrupt

claim: Closed-loop verification (test before final action) and self-verifying domains enable agents to deliver correct results without human feedback loops
actionable: Design agent tooling for verifiable domains (SQL, code, math); include test/check tools before final action; agents can self-verify correctness

claim: O3 mini model in architect mode with high reasoning effort + Claude 3.5 Sonnet editor provides best cost-quality balance for AI coding refactoring
actionable: For AI coding refactoring tasks, use O3 mini (architect reasoning) to plan changes and Claude 3.5 Sonnet (editor) to implement—balance cost and quality

claim: AI coding (writing code with models) is now a critical skill parity with traditional coding; engineers not using AI coding tools are at productivity disadvantage
actionable: If you are writing code manually, adopt AI coding tools (Cursor, Aider, Claude Code); this is now baseline expectation for competitive engineering in 2025
